text:
Market today: Mixed-to-cautious mood. Micron's blowout earnings are the real driver — chip stocks getting a lift, but Apple dropping 6% on memory-cost pain is a reminder that component inflation cuts both ways. Your tech-heavy, margin-loaded book is caught in that crossfire.

Your holdings: PLTR is your most urgent problem — down 17.6% and flagged SELL; on margin that hole gets expensive fast, stop at $85 gives you more room but the signal says act. AVGO is your largest position, down 12.9% and flagged TRIM — Apple's memory crunch headwind is real for a chip supplier; size this down. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no trading history — tiny move to your $150 stop, treat it as fragile. Margin amplifies every one of these drops.

Watch: Whether today's Micron bounce carries into SMH and lifts AVGO off the mat — or fades by close. Apple's 6% drop and where it stabilizes; it signals how bad the memory-cost story really gets.

Bottom line: Cut or trim your losers on margin before the market decides for you.
